verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the β subunit of ATP synthase, a core component of the F₁ catalytic head of the mitochondrial ATP synthase complex. This protein is synthesized on mitochondrial ribosomes, remains tightly integrated into the F₁ sector of ATP synthase anchored to the inner mitochondrial membrane, and faces the matrix side. It has no transmembrane domains, no signal peptide for secretion, no documented lipidation, no evidence of trafficking to the PM under any cell state, and no known mechanisms for lysosomal exocytosis delivery to the cell surface. No therapeutic programs target ATP5F1B on the cell surface. Ruling out contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced—no stress, death, or activation pathway induces PM translocation of mitochondrial matrix-facing proteins; (2) tissue_restricted_surface—not applicable, protein is not surface-displaying in any lineage; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis—mitochondrial inner-membrane proteins are not lysosomal cargo; (4) dual_localization—no documented steady-state or cycling PM pool; (5) stable_surface_attachment—not secreted or cleaved to yield a surface-anchored product. ATP5F1B is definitionally mitochondrial-internal, matrix-facing.
